claims_supported: NIST evaluated 1000-to-500 PPI fingerprint downsampling methods
Gaussian low-pass filtering with sigma 0.8475 and radius 4 followed by decimation was selected
SP 500-289 specifies odd-column and odd-row decimation
edge handling in the study mirrors or duplicates edge pixels
1000_ppi_sources.limitations: A research report about 1000-to-500 PPI only; it is not evidence for any 2000 PPI path.
1000_ppi_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
1000_ppi_sources.relevance: Method selection, kernel parameters, edge behavior, and decimation study
1000_ppi_sources.title: Examination of Downsampling Strategies for Converting 1000 ppi Fingerprint Imagery to 500 ppi

claims_supported: NIST guidance defines a 1000-to-500 PPI interoperability path
The selected method uses sigma 0.8475, radius 4, and odd-column/odd-row decimation
1000_ppi_sources.limitations: Does not place a downsampler implementation in NBIS 5.0.0 and does not address 2000 PPI.
1000_ppi_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
1000_ppi_sources.relevance: Normative NIST compression and interoperability guidance
1000_ppi_sources.title: Compression Guidance for 1000 ppi Friction Ridge Imagery

claims_supported: NIST defines a conformance pathway for supplier implementations of the 1000-to-500 PPI method
The procedure compares outputs to a NIST reference downsampler
1000_ppi_sources.limitations: Published after NBIS 5.0.0; mentioning a reference downsampler does not prove that implementation is in the 5.0.0 archive.
1000_ppi_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
1000_ppi_sources.relevance: Independent conformance evidence and implementation identity requirements
1000_ppi_sources.title: Certification Pathway for Downsampling 1000 ppi Fingerprint Friction Ridge Imagery to 500 ppi

claims_supported: The reference data preparation used scans at 500, 1000, and 2000 PPI to align corresponding regions
The conformance procedure itself is scoped to 1000-to-500 PPI
2000_ppi_search_sources.limitations: A 2000 PPI scan used in reference-data alignment is not approval for direct matching, 2000-to-1000, 2000-to-500, or a cascade.
2000_ppi_search_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
2000_ppi_search_sources.relevance: Bounds what the official certification document does and does not establish
2000_ppi_search_sources.title: Certification Pathway for Downsampling 1000 ppi Fingerprint Friction Ridge Imagery to 500 ppi

claims_supported: BOZORTH3 accepts MINDTCT minutiae output
BOZORTH3 is the NBIS minutiae matcher
bozorth3_sources.limitations: Product-level description; detailed command and output semantics were audited in the acquired source tree.
bozorth3_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
bozorth3_sources.title: NIST Biometric Image Software (NBIS)

claims_supported: NBIS was developed using gcc and gmake
NIST reports testing on Linux, macOS, and Cygwin
build_sources.limitations: No supported build environment was already present on the audit host.
build_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
build_sources.title: NIST Biometric Image Software (NBIS)

claims_supported: MINDTCT detects minutiae and produces minutiae data usable by BOZORTH3
mindtct_sources.limitations: The guide predates release 5.0.0; release-specific behavior was checked against the acquired 5.0.0 source.
mindtct_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
mindtct_sources.title: User's Guide to NIST Biometric Image Software (NBIS)

claims_supported: The official stable release is NBIS 5.0.0
The official archive is hosted by NIST
The release date is 2015-03-04
software_identity_sources.limitations: Release listing and archive link only.
software_identity_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
software_identity_sources.relevance: Release identity and official acquisition endpoint
software_identity_sources.title: NIST Image Group Open Source Server (NIGOS)

claims_supported: NBIS is provided by NIST as public-domain software
MINDTCT and BOZORTH3 are NBIS components
software_identity_sources.limitations: The page is descriptive; exact source provenance is separately locked by archive hash.
software_identity_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
software_identity_sources.relevance: Identity, licensing, component scope, and build overview
software_identity_sources.title: NIST Biometric Image Software (NBIS)
